plan: 1. Create pure draw logic in `src/domain/drawRandom.ts`: implement a `createDrawPool(cards: Card[]): DrawPool` factory and a `drawOne(pool: DrawPool): { card: Card; pool: DrawPool }` function. DrawPool tracks available card indices and drawn card indices. When available pool is exhausted, return indicator so UI can prompt or auto-reset. Include a `resetPool(cards: Card[]): DrawPool` function. All functions are pure (no side effects) and testable — randomization is injected or uses Math.random internally but the pool logic is deterministic given a pick.

2. Write unit tests in `tests/unit/draw-random.test.ts`: test that drawOne returns a valid card and removes it from available pool; test that all 57 cards are drawn before any repeat (full cycle guarantee); test that drawing from an exhausted pool returns an exhaustion signal; test that resetPool restores the full deck; test edge cases (single-card deck, two-card deck).

3. Create the `SingleDraw` React component in `src/features/play/SingleDraw.tsx`: imports `cardDeck` from data layer and uses `drawRandom` domain logic via a `useReducer` or `useState` hook. Renders a 'Draw' button, the currently drawn `CardVisual` (if any), a card counter showing 'X of 57 remaining', and a 'Reset' button. The Draw button is disabled when pool is exhausted (with a message prompting reset).

4. Create `src/features/play/SingleDraw.module.css` for layout styling: centered draw area, spacing for the button and card display, subtle entrance transition for drawn card (keep it minimal per CLAUDE.md), responsive layout.

5. Wire the SingleDraw component into the app: update `src/features/play/index.ts` to export `SingleDraw`. Since App.tsx currently has no routing and browse feature is also a stub, create a minimal hash-based page switcher in App.tsx (read `window.location.hash`) to render SingleDraw when hash is `#/play` — or if a router is already planned for a different ticket, just mount SingleDraw at the `/play` path. Update Nav links if needed to use hash routes.

6. Write component tests in `tests/unit/single-draw.test.tsx`: test that Draw button renders and is clickable; test that clicking Draw shows a CardVisual; test that clicking Draw repeatedly never shows the same card twice; test that Reset button appears and resets the pool; test that Draw button disables when deck is exhausted.
7. Run `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run test` to validate all checks pass. Fix any issues found.
8. Optionally run `npm run test:e2e` if Playwright smoke tests cover the play route.
testing_recommendations: Unit test drawOne for full-cycle guarantee: draw all 57 cards, assert no duplicates in the sequence
Unit test exhaustion: after 57 draws, drawOne signals pool empty
Unit test resetPool: after reset, full 57 cards are available again
Unit test edge case: single-card pool draws that card, then exhausts
Component test: clicking Draw renders a CardVisual with valid card data
Component test: clicking Draw 57 times never renders duplicate cardNumbers
Component test: Reset button re-enables drawing after exhaustion
Component test: Draw button is disabled (or hidden) when pool is exhausted
Run npm run lint, npm run typecheck, npm run test before marking complete
risks: No routing exists yet — App.tsx is a static shell. US-014 needs a minimal page-switching mechanism to mount the play view. This could be a simple hash router or deferred if another ticket handles routing. Decision needed.

Math.random is not seedable — for deterministic unit tests, the draw function should accept an optional random source or tests should verify statistical properties (no repeats, full cycle) rather than exact sequences.

The browse feature (US-007/US-008 dependencies) is also a stub export — confirming those tickets only delivered domain/data layer (not routing) means US-014 may be the first ticket to introduce page navigation.
